entries.d: To decide what to do after thinking about it
entries.s: Have you _____ up your mind yet about which new T-shirt you want to buy — the blue one or the red one?

entries.d: To wait until the next day before making a decision
entries.s: I'm not sure yet what to do about the big decision. I think I'll _____ and decide in the morning when I'm less tired.

entries.w: to find a way out of a dilemma
entries.g: einen Ausweg aus einem Dilemma finden
entries.d: To discover a solution to a difficult problem
entries.s: Let's try to _____ a way out of this really difficult situation together by brainstorming possible solutions.
a: find a way out of a dilemma

entries.w: to be in two minds about sth.
entries.d: To be unsure because you are thinking both yes and no
entries.s: I'm _____ two minds about going to Anna's birthday party tonight — part of me wants to go, part of me wants to stay home.

entries.w: to have second thoughts about sth.
entries.d: To start thinking that maybe your first idea wasn't good
entries.s: I _____ second thoughts about buying this very expensive new phone because it costs a lot more than I can afford right now.

entries.d: To not know what to do or say
entries.s: When her friend got terrible news she stood completely _____, with no idea what to say or do.
mc: to know exactly what to do

entries.d: A difficult situation where you must choose between two things
entries.s: It's a real _____ — I want to go to both parties tonight but they are at exactly the same time in different places.

entries.d: To finally decide something after thinking about it
entries.s: After a long discussion for two hours, they finally _____ a decision to go on holiday to Italy instead of Spain.

entries.d: To say that something planned will not happen
entries.s: We had to _____ the outdoor picnic at the last minute because of the sudden heavy thunderstorm that arrived.

entries.d: A feeling of sadness when something is not as good as you hoped
entries.s: Not getting the job she had applied for was a big _____ for her — she had really wanted it and prepared hard for the interview.

entries.d: Your grandfather, your parent's father
entries.s: My old _____ — my dad's father — always tells us funny stories from when he was young boy during the war.

entries.d: To force someone to leave a team or group
entries.s: The strict football coach will _____ the team if you miss practice again without a good reason like illness.

entries.d: To change the time or date of something (in this context)
entries.s: Can we _____ the important class meeting from Wednesday afternoon to Thursday morning instead, because of the school trip?

entries.d: To think about something again because you might change your mind
entries.s: Maybe I should _____ my original holiday plan and try something completely different this year, like going camping instead of to the beach.

entries.d: To have earned something good (or bad) because of what you did
entries.s: She worked so incredibly hard all year on her homework and studies — she really _____ the top grade on her final report.

entries.d: Not including, but not
entries.s: Everyone in our class of 25 students is here today _____ Tom — he's still at home sick with the flu.

entries.d: A way to say you feel sorry about something
entries.s: She didn't win the first place prize after all her hard work and months of preparation. _____.

entries.d: A free ride in someone's car
entries.s: Can you give me a _____ to the train station in your car? I'm in a hurry and it's too far to walk in time.

entries.d: Proof that you were somewhere else when something happened
entries.s: The police officers asked the suspect for a proper _____ — proof of where he was during the time of the crime last night.

entries.d: A card or document that shows who you are
entries.s: You need to show your official _____ card with your photo and name to get into the concert venue at the entrance.

entries.d: To not say anything about something
entries.s: He decided to _____ about what he saw and not tell anyone, even though he knew he had witnessed the incident clearly.

entries.d: To report to a teacher or parent what someone did wrong
entries.s: Please don't _____ on me to the teacher — I promise I won't break the classroom rules about phones again, it was just one time.

entries.d: A punishment where you have to stay at school after classes end
entries.s: He got _____ for one hour after school for talking loudly during class again, even after the teacher had warned him twice.

entries.d: To hang or fix something on a wall or board
entries.s: The art teacher _____ our colourful drawings of animals on the classroom wall last week for everyone to see.

entries.d: To invite someone on a date
entries.s: He was too shy to _____ her on a date for coffee at the café — he had liked her secretly for months but couldn't find the courage.

entries.d: Without meaning to do it, by mistake
entries.s: I _____ deleted all my favourite photos from my phone last night — I didn't mean to do it at all, my finger slipped on the button!

entries.d: To have a fight with words, to disagree strongly
entries.s: My little brother and I sometimes _____ about which TV show to watch on Saturday mornings — he wants cartoons and I want quiz shows.

entries.d: To pretend you don't see something wrong
entries.s: When she saw the boy being bullied by two older students, she _____ instead of helping — she pretended not to see.

entries.d: To act as if something is true when it is not
entries.s: She _____ to be completely asleep in bed so that she didn't have to talk to her annoying aunt who was visiting.

entries.d: To say no to something or someone
entries.s: The strict teacher _____ my excuse for not doing homework because it wasn't believable — I said my dog ate it but I don't have a dog.

entries.d: Made at home, not bought in a shop
entries.s: My grandma's delicious _____ chocolate cake baked in her own kitchen is much better than any cake you can buy from a bakery.

entries.d: Not one and not the other of two things or people
entries.s: _____ us wanted to go into the dark haunted house first, so we waited outside for a whole minute arguing about who should go.

entries.d: A piece of paper that you can use instead of money
entries.s: She got a paper €20 _____ for the big bookshop as a generous birthday present from her godmother last week.

entries.d: To cover something with paper or cloth
entries.s: I need to carefully _____ this present in colourful paper with a ribbon before the birthday party starts at three o'clock.
